essence: Leads CNC teams on the shop floor, coordinating workflow, solving machining issues, and ensuring high‑quality production output.

identity.title: CNC Team Leader
identity.summary: Supervises CNC operators, setters, and machinists; manages daily workflow; resolves technical issues; and ensures production targets, quality standards, and safety requirements are met.

salary.notes: Team leaders in aerospace, defence, or multi‑cell machining environments can exceed £72k-£90k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, setting, or programming
Knowledge of tooling, materials, tolerances, and machining processes
Ability to lead teams and coordinate workflow
Understanding of quality control, safety standards, and lean manufacturing

lifestyle.description: CNC team leaders work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They coordinate teams, solve machining issues, and ensure production runs smoothly. The role suits individuals who enjoy leadership, technical expertise, and improving manufacturing performance.

habits: Leads CNC operators, setters, and machinists
Ensures quality standards and tolerances are met
Maintains safety, documentation, and process efficiency
